channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 To Handle Complex Jobs On Its Own

media:content.media:description: Claude Sonnet 5 is becoming the default model for users on Claude's Free and Pro plans

item.description: The Claude Sonnet 5 launch by Anthropic reflects a broader shift taking place across the AI industry.
item.content:encoded: The Claude Sonnet 5 launch by Anthropic reflects a broader shift taking place across the AI industry.

item.title: 'World Order Will Collapse': Pakistan's Cry For Help Over Indus Waters

media:content.media:description: Indus Waters Treaty regulates the distribution of water from the Indus River system

item.description: One of the strongest remarks came from Pakistani Minister Musadik Malik, who claimed that the future of the treaty would determine the credibility of international agreements worldwide.
item.content:encoded: One of the strongest remarks came from Pakistani Minister Musadik Malik, who claimed that the future of the treaty would determine the credibility of international agreements worldwide.

item.title: US Removes 4 Indian Firms From Sanctions List Over Russia Trade

media:content.media:description: The US has removed four Indian companies from its sanctions list.

item.description: The US had sanctioned Galaxy Bearings Ltd in October 2024, accusing it of exporting dozens of high-priority dual-use equipment, including roller bearings and roller assemblies, to Russian entities.
item.content:encoded: The US had sanctioned Galaxy Bearings Ltd in October 2024, accusing it of exporting dozens of high-priority dual-use equipment, including roller bearings and roller assemblies, to Russian entities.

item.title: Ukraine's Missiles Now Have Almost Half Of Russia Within Reach

media:content.media:description: Ukraine is increasingly relying on domestically developed long range cruise missiles.

item.description: Ukraine has deployed new long-range Flamingo and Neptune missiles, triggering alerts across half of Russia and striking targets over 1,500 km away.
item.content:encoded: Ukraine has deployed new long-range Flamingo and Neptune missiles, triggering alerts across half of Russia and striking targets over 1,500 km away.

item.title: Japan To Invest $6 Billion In AI, Have 10 Million AI Robots By 2040

media:content.media:description: A humanoid robot sits between stools at an exhibition in Tokyo. (File)

item.description: Countries around the world are seeking to develop sovereign AI models to reduce a potentially dangerous over-reliance on technology from the United States and China.
item.content:encoded: Countries around the world are seeking to develop sovereign AI models to reduce a potentially dangerous over-reliance on technology from the United States and China.

item.title: Global Oceans Break June Heat Record At 20.98 Degrees Celsius

media:content.media:description: Onset of a potentially powerful El Nino weather pattern could boost global heat in the oceans: Scientists

item.description: Global average sea surface temperatures in June were 20.98C, beating the previous records of 2023 and 2024, according to the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service.
item.content:encoded: Global average sea surface temperatures in June were 20.98C, beating the previous records of 2023 and 2024, according to the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service.

item.title: "I'm Going To Sleep": Woman's 48 Hours Under The Rubble In Venezuela

media:content.media:description: Andrea Canonico, 23, spent almost 48 hours in the same position before being pulled out -- alive.

item.description: "I had my phone, of course," which allowed 23-year-old Andrea Canonico to keep track of time and enjoy a little light.
item.content:encoded: "I had my phone, of course," which allowed 23-year-old Andrea Canonico to keep track of time and enjoy a little light.

item.title: Venezuela Earthquake Survivors Rely On Kindness Of Strangers, Neighbours

media:content.media:description: Twin earthquakes in Venezuela killed almost 2,000 people and affected over 15,000 more.

item.description: Volunteers drive relief efforts in devastated Venezuela, providing food and care as survivors slam government inaction.
item.content:encoded: Volunteers drive relief efforts in devastated Venezuela, providing food and care as survivors slam government inaction.

item.title: Brain, Heart, Lungs Missing From Indian Sailor's Body Who Died In Venezuela, Family Demands Probe

media:content.media:description: According to his family, they received news of his death from his company

item.description: FSUI noted that Chauhan's remains were sent to his hometown in Uttar Pradesh's Deoria without any autopsy report or details from Venezuelan authorities.
item.content:encoded: FSUI noted that Chauhan's remains were sent to his hometown in Uttar Pradesh's Deoria without any autopsy report or details from Venezuelan authorities.

item.title: Bangladeshi Media Told Not To Report Sheikh Hasina's Remarks After NDTV Interview

media:content.media:description: Sheikh Hasina was hounded out of Bangladesh in 2024 following violent protests.

item.description: According to reports, Bangladeshi PM's Information and Broadcasting Adviser, Zahed Ur Rahman, told the media outlets that reporting on Hasina's remarks is "completely prohibited".
item.content:encoded: According to reports, Bangladeshi PM's Information and Broadcasting Adviser, Zahed Ur Rahman, told the media outlets that reporting on Hasina's remarks is "completely prohibited".

item.title: China's New Tech Turns Seawater Into Drinking Water, No Power Needed

media:content.media:description: The water met WHO drinking-water standards.

item.description: Chinese researchers have created a solar-powered desalination material that uses nanoparticle technology to convert seawater into fresh water at a cost lower than bottled water, running entirely on sunlight with no external power needed.
item.content:encoded: Chinese researchers have created a solar-powered desalination material that uses nanoparticle technology to convert seawater into fresh water at a cost lower than bottled water, running entirely on sunlight with no external power needed.

item.title: Why US Supreme Court Birthright Citizenship Ruling Is A Relief For Indians

media:content.media:description: In the late 1800s, birthright citizenship was legally expanded to the children of immigrants.

item.description: Hundreds of thousands of Indian professionals work and study in the country on H-1B visas, L-1 intra-company transfer visas, and F-1 student visas, while more than a million Indians remain in the employment-based green card backlog
item.content:encoded: Hundreds of thousands of Indian professionals work and study in the country on H-1B visas, L-1 intra-company transfer visas, and F-1 student visas, while more than a million Indians remain in the employment-based green card backlog

item.title: "Microsoft Powered Genocide": Employee Resigns, Sends Mass Email

media:content.media:description: Microsoft has rejected claims that its services are used to harm civilians.

item.description: A former Microsoft data centre technician in Italy has resigned and publicly accused the company of supporting Israeli military operations through its cloud infrastructure and European data centres.
item.content:encoded: A former Microsoft data centre technician in Italy has resigned and publicly accused the company of supporting Israeli military operations through its cloud infrastructure and European data centres.

item.title: 'Can Bring In Legislation': Trump On Supreme Court's Citizenship Ruling

media:content.media:description: The Supreme Court agreed in a majority opinion penned by Chief Justice John Roberts

item.description: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump wrote that the Congress should start working "today" to end the "expensive and unfair" birthright citizenship.
item.content:encoded: In a post on X, US President Donald Trump wrote that the Congress should start working "today" to end the "expensive and unfair" birthright citizenship.

item.title: 11-Year-Old Canadian Boy Dies Of Rabies After Waking Up With Bat On His Face

media:content.media:description: His parents agreed to share his story to raise awareness about rabies.

item.description: Because the boy had no apparent bite or scratch marks and appeared perfectly healthy, his family did not seek medical care.
item.content:encoded: Because the boy had no apparent bite or scratch marks and appeared perfectly healthy, his family did not seek medical care.

item.title: 25-Year-Old Hindu Priest 'Kidnapped', 'Tortured' For Ransom In Bangladesh

media:content.media:description: It was not immediately clear if Deuri was targeted because of his faith.

item.description: Deuri -- a first-year student at Central Law College in Old Dhaka, who also occasionally serves as an assistant priest at the central temple of Jagannath University -- was targeted by unidentified persons late on Monday.
item.content:encoded: Deuri -- a first-year student at Central Law College in Old Dhaka, who also occasionally serves as an assistant priest at the central temple of Jagannath University -- was targeted by unidentified persons late on Monday.

item.title: Airlines Baggage Mishandling Down By 23%, Still Costs $6.3 Billion: Report

media:content.media:description: Baggage mishandling rate fell 23 per cent to 4.9 per 1,000 passengers in 2025.

item.description: With net profit averaging just $8 per passenger, one mishandled bag wipes out the profit from more than 30 seats sold, the report said.
item.content:encoded: With net profit averaging just $8 per passenger, one mishandled bag wipes out the profit from more than 30 seats sold, the report said.

item.title: US Lifts Restrictions On Anthropic's Fable 5, Mythos 5 AI Models

media:content.media:description: Earlier this month, US authorities blocked access to the models on national security grounds.

item.description: Anthropic will soon begin restoring access globally to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government lifted a restriction on where they could be released, the company said Tuesday.
item.content:encoded: Anthropic will soon begin restoring access globally to its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the US government lifted a restriction on where they could be released, the company said Tuesday.

item.title: Microsoft To Cut Under 2.5% Of Workforce In Latest Layoffs: Report

media:content.media:description: In July 2025, the company said it would lay off nearly 4% of its workforce.

item.description: Microsoft is planning to cut under 2.5% of its workforce in the latest round of layoffs that could be announced as early as next week, Business Insider reported on Tuesday, citing sources.
item.content:encoded: Microsoft is planning to cut under 2.5% of its workforce in the latest round of layoffs that could be announced as early as next week, Business Insider reported on Tuesday, citing sources.

item.title: 3-Year-Old Rescued From Collapsed Building 6 Days After Venezuela Quakes

media:content.media:description: A child receives emergency medical attention after being rescued by rescuers from rubble.

item.description: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 less than a minute apart last Wednesday, toppling buildings and trapping thousands of people beneath the rubble, according to authorities and rescue teams.
item.content:encoded: Venezuela was hit by two earthquakes of magnitudes 7.2 and 7.5 less than a minute apart last Wednesday, toppling buildings and trapping thousands of people beneath the rubble, according to authorities and rescue teams.
